(a) The driver of a vehicle shall not back the same unless such movement can be made with safety and without interfering with other traffic.

(b) The driver of a vehicle shall not back the same upon any shoulder or roadway of any controlled-access highway.

(c) the driver of a motor vehicle engaged in retail sales of frozen desserts as that term is defined in subdivision thirty-seven of § 375 (Equipment) directly to pedestrians shall not back the same to make or attempt to make a sale.
markdown:
# Vehicle & Traffic Law
## Title 7
#### Article 33. Misc. Rules
##### § 1211 — Limitations on backing

(a) The driver of a vehicle shall not back the same unless such movement can be made with safety and without interfering with other traffic.

(b) The driver of a vehicle shall not back the same upon any shoulder or roadway of any controlled-access highway.

(c) the driver of a motor vehicle engaged in retail sales of frozen desserts as that term is defined in subdivision thirty-seven of § 375 (Equipment) directly to pedestrians shall not back the same to make or attempt to make a sale.

*Last modified: Sep. 22, 2014*
